pattern: Noun / V dict + に難くない
meaning_en: easy to imagine / not hard to
meaning_detailed: 〜に難くない is a literary construction meaning 'not hard to X' — typically used with verbs of imagination, understanding, or inference to indicate that the main clause situation is readily graspable. Common fixed forms: 想像に難くない ('easy to imagine'), 察するに難くない ('easy to infer'). Very formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + に難くない; V dict-form + に難くない.
formation_notes: Literary / formal 'not hard to X'.
Most common fixed form: 想像に難くない ('easy to imagine').
Other common: 察するに難くない, 理解するに難くない.
Rare in casual speech — typical of essays, editorials, and formal writing.

examples.japanese: 彼の苦労は想像に難くない。
examples.english: His hardship is easy to imagine.

examples.japanese: 結果は予想するに難くない。
examples.english: The result is not hard to predict.

examples.japanese: 親の気持ちは察するに難くない。
examples.english: A parent's feelings are easy to infer.

pattern: Noun + を余儀なくされる
meaning_en: be forced to / have no choice but to
meaning_detailed: 〜を余儀なくされる expresses that someone is forced into a situation by external circumstance — 'be compelled to X', 'have no choice but to X'. Formal / written register, common in news reports and formal narratives. The corresponding active 〜を余儀なくさせる means 'to force someone to X'.
formation: Noun + を余儀なくされる (passive). Active variant: 〜を余儀なくさせる.
formation_notes: Passive — something / circumstances force the subject into X.
Formal / news register — common in reports of crisis or change.
Common nouns: 中止 ('cancellation'), 変更 ('change'), 撤退 ('withdrawal'), 辞任 ('resignation').
The active voice 〜を余儀なくさせる is less common but used in the same register.

examples.japanese: 台風のため、会議の中止を余儀なくされた。
examples.english: Due to the typhoon, we were forced to cancel the meeting.

examples.japanese: 経営悪化で、社長は辞任を余儀なくされた。
examples.english: Due to poor management, the president was forced to resign.

examples.japanese: 怪我で、計画変更を余儀なくされた。
examples.english: Due to injury, we had no choice but to change the plan.

pattern: Noun (emotion) + を禁じ得ない
meaning_en: cannot suppress (an emotion) / can't help feeling
meaning_detailed: 〜を禁じ得ない expresses that the speaker cannot suppress or hold back a strong emotion — 'cannot help feeling X'. Typically used with emotion nouns: 怒り ('anger'), 涙 ('tears'), 驚き ('surprise'), 同情 ('sympathy'). Very formal / literary register, common in speeches and formal prose.
formation: Noun (emotion) + を禁じ得ない.
formation_notes: Very formal / literary — expresses overwhelming, uncontrollable emotion.
Common emotion nouns: 怒り, 涙, 驚き, 同情, 失望.
The verb 禁じる literally means 'to prohibit / suppress' — 得ない adds 'cannot'.
Rare in casual speech — typical of formal reports and reflective writing.

examples.japanese: その話を聞いて、涙を禁じ得なかった。
examples.english: Hearing the story, I couldn't hold back my tears.

examples.japanese: 彼の態度に、怒りを禁じ得ない。
examples.english: I cannot suppress my anger at his attitude.

examples.japanese: 被害者に対して同情を禁じ得ない。
examples.english: I can't help feeling sympathy for the victims.

pattern: V negative stem + ずにはおかない
meaning_en: will not leave without doing / inevitably will
meaning_detailed: 〜ずにはおかない expresses determined resolve or natural inevitability — 'I won't rest until X is done' / 'X will inevitably happen'. Two senses: (1) speaker resolve, and (2) natural consequence. Formal / literary register. Irregular: する → せずにはおかない.
formation: V ない-stem + ずにはおかない. Irregular: する → せずにはおかない.
formation_notes: Two senses: determined resolve (will definitely do X) or natural consequence (X will inevitably follow).
Irregular: せずにはおかない for する.
Compare with 〜ずにはいられない (can't help doing, involuntary emotion).
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: この事実を世に知らしめずにはおかない。
examples.english: I will not rest without making this fact known to the world.

examples.japanese: 彼の発言は波紋を広げずにはおかない。
examples.english: His remarks will inevitably cause ripples.

examples.japanese: 真実を明らかにせずにはおかない。
examples.english: I will not stop until the truth is made clear.

pattern: V ない-form + ではおかない
meaning_en: will not fail to / will inevitably
meaning_detailed: 〜ないではおかない is a less literary variant of 〜ずにはおかない, expressing determined action or natural inevitability — 'will not leave without X happening'. Formal register, interchangeable in meaning with ずにはおかない. The form uses the casual negative ない-form with ではおかない.
formation: V ない-form + ではおかない.
formation_notes: Less formal variant of 〜ずにはおかない — same two senses (resolve / inevitability).
The negative form attaches directly: 言わない → 言わないではおかない.
Formal register — rare in casual speech.
Compare with 〜ないではいられない (involuntary emotion, more common).

examples.japanese: 彼に一言言わないではおかない。
examples.english: I will not leave without saying a word to him.

examples.japanese: この発見は歴史を変えないではおかない。
examples.english: This discovery will inevitably change history.

examples.japanese: 真相を明らかにしないではおかない。
examples.english: I won't rest until the truth is revealed.

pattern: Noun + にもまして
meaning_en: more than / even more than (emphatic)
meaning_detailed: 〜にもまして expresses that something exceeds a reference in degree — 'even more than X'. Often in comparative statements where the speaker wants to emphasize that the main subject surpasses the compared standard. Literary / formal register.
formation: Noun + にもまして.
formation_notes: Comparative — exceeds the standard in the noun.
Often with time references: 以前にもまして ('more than before'), 去年にもまして ('more than last year').
Also with qualities: 誰にもまして ('more than anyone').
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: 以前にもまして、忙しくなった。
examples.english: I've become busier than before.

examples.japanese: 今年は去年にもまして暑い。
examples.english: This year is even hotter than last year.

examples.japanese: 誰にもまして、彼女が頑張っている。
examples.english: More than anyone, she's working hard.

pattern: Noun + をものともせず(に)
meaning_en: braving / undaunted by / in defiance of
meaning_detailed: 〜をものともせず expresses that the subject acts 'undaunted by' or 'in defiance of' X — often a hardship, danger, or obstacle. Carries an admiring tone for the subject's courage or determination. Formal / literary register, common in heroic narratives and tributes.
formation: Noun + をものともせず(に) + main clause (positive / determined action).
formation_notes: Admiring tone — the subject perseveres despite the obstacle.
Common nouns: 困難 ('difficulty'), 危険 ('danger'), 反対 ('opposition'), 雨 ('rain').
Formal / literary register — common in biographies and praise.
Compare with 〜をよそに (disregard, sometimes critical) — ものともせず is admiring.

examples.japanese: 彼は困難をものともせず、目標を達成した。
examples.english: Undaunted by difficulties, he achieved his goal.

examples.japanese: 大雨をものともせず、試合は続けられた。
examples.english: Braving the heavy rain, the match continued.

examples.japanese: 反対をものともせず、彼は自分の道を進んだ。
examples.english: In defiance of opposition, he followed his own path.

pattern: Noun + (の) + いかんによらず / いかんを問わず
meaning_en: regardless of / irrespective of (formal)
meaning_detailed: 〜いかんによらず and 〜いかんを問わず are formal variants of 〜にかかわらず — 'regardless of X'. Very formal, common in legal, official, and business contexts. Builds on the formal いかん ('how / depending on').
formation: Noun + (の) + いかんによらず / いかんを問わず / いかんにかかわらず.
formation_notes: Very formal — common in legal, governmental, and official documents.
Variants: いかんによらず, いかんを問わず, いかんにかかわらず.
Based on いかん (literary 'how / depending on').
Less common in casual speech — use にかかわらず instead.

examples.japanese: 理由のいかんによらず、遅刻は許されない。
examples.english: Regardless of the reason, tardiness is not allowed.

examples.japanese: 結果のいかんを問わず、挑戦する。
examples.english: Regardless of the outcome, I will try.

examples.japanese: 性別のいかんにかかわらず、誰でも応募できる。
examples.english: Regardless of gender, anyone can apply.

pattern: Noun + ともあろう + noun
meaning_en: of all people / someone as prominent as
meaning_detailed: 〜ともあろう expresses astonishment or criticism that someone of the X category (usually someone expected to behave well) has done something unbefitting — 'of all people, X did Y'. Often with a critical or disappointed tone. Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun (role / title) + ともあろう + noun (often person: 者, 人, 男, 女).
formation_notes: Critical / disappointed — 'someone of X's stature should not have done Y'.
Common with prominent roles: 先生, 大人, 社長, 医者, 警察官.
Formal / literary register — common in criticism and editorials.
Followed by a noun describing the person, often 者 or 人.

examples.japanese: 先生ともあろう者が、嘘をつくとは。
examples.english: For a teacher of all people to tell a lie!

examples.japanese: 大人ともあろう者が、そんな行動をとるべきではない。
examples.english: A grown adult shouldn't behave that way.

examples.japanese: 社長ともあろう人が、約束を破るなんて。
examples.english: For the president, of all people, to break his word!

pattern: Noun + ならでは(の)
meaning_en: unique to / distinctive of / only X has
meaning_detailed: 〜ならでは expresses that something is uniquely characteristic of or possible only for X — 'distinctive to X' / 'only X has this'. Commonly used in marketing, tourism, and praise contexts to highlight unique features. The attributive form ならではの + noun is the most common usage.
formation: Noun + ならではの + noun (attributive); noun + ならでは (predicate).
formation_notes: Positive / unique — highlights distinctiveness.
Common in tourism, marketing, reviews: 日本ならではの, 京都ならではの.
Often praised for authentic / unique features.
Formal / literary register but widely used in everyday praise.

examples.japanese: 京都ならではの美しい景色だ。
examples.english: It's beautiful scenery unique to Kyoto.

examples.japanese: 子供ならではの発想が面白い。
examples.english: The ideas unique to children are interesting.

examples.japanese: この味は日本ならではだ。
examples.english: This flavor is distinctively Japanese.

pattern: Noun + にあって
meaning_en: in / at / during (formal literary)
meaning_detailed: 〜にあって is a formal / literary marker meaning 'in' or 'at' a particular time, place, or situation — often emphasizing the significance of that setting. Close to 〜において but with a more situational / circumstantial feel. Common in historical narratives and formal essays.
formation: Noun + にあって.
formation_notes: Formal / literary 'in / during' — emphasizes setting or situation.
Close to 〜において but slightly more situational.
Common with period / situation nouns: 現代にあって, 混乱にあって, 困難な状況にあって.
Very formal — rare in casual speech.

examples.japanese: このグローバル時代にあって、英語は必須だ。
examples.english: In this global age, English is essential.

examples.japanese: 困難な状況にあって、彼は冷静を保った。
examples.english: In a difficult situation, he kept calm.

examples.japanese: 平和な時代にあっても、危機管理は必要だ。
examples.english: Even in peaceful times, crisis management is necessary.

pattern: Noun + たる者
meaning_en: as a (proper) X, one should / one who is X
meaning_detailed: 〜たる者 expresses 'as a (proper) X, one should Y' — a moralizing statement about the expected behavior or responsibilities of someone in the X role. The main clause typically expresses an obligation or standard. Very formal / literary, classical-sounding register.
formation: Noun (role) + たる者 + normative predicate.
formation_notes: Classical / literary — archaic feel.
Common roles: 男たる者, 教師たる者, 政治家たる者, リーダーたる者.
Main clause is a normative / moral statement: 〜すべきだ, 〜ねばならない.
Rare in casual speech — common in moralizing rhetoric.

examples.japanese: リーダーたる者、部下の模範となるべきだ。
examples.english: As a leader, one should be a model for subordinates.

examples.japanese: 教師たる者、常に学び続けなければならない。
examples.english: As a teacher, one must always keep learning.

examples.japanese: 武士たる者、名誉を重んじるべきだ。
examples.english: As a samurai, one should value honor.

pattern: Noun/V + なり + noun/V + なり
meaning_en: either X or Y / whether X or Y
meaning_detailed: 〜なり〜なり lists options, suggesting 'X or Y (or something like them)' — 'either X or Y'. Often used for practical suggestions about ways to handle a situation. The listed options are alternatives the listener can choose among. Neutral / formal register.
formation: Noun + なり + noun + なり; V dict-form + なり + V dict-form + なり.
formation_notes: Option listing — 'X or Y, take your pick'.
Often in practical suggestions: 電話するなりメールするなり, connect to.
Distinct from other なり senses (as soon as, in one's way).
Formal / neutral register — common in practical advice.

examples.japanese: 電話するなりメールするなり、連絡してください。
examples.english: Contact me by phone or email, whichever.

examples.japanese: 親に相談するなり、先生に聞くなりしなさい。
examples.english: Talk to your parents or ask your teacher, whichever.

examples.japanese: 休むなり、薬を飲むなりしたほうがいい。
examples.english: You should rest, or take medicine, one or the other.

pattern: Noun + といわず + noun + といわず
meaning_en: whether X or Y / all over / throughout
meaning_detailed: 〜といわず〜といわず expresses that something applies comprehensively to both X and Y (and by implication, everything in between) — 'X, Y, you name it' / 'all over'. Literary / emphatic register, often used for thoroughness or comprehensive description.
formation: Noun + といわず + noun + といわず + main clause.
formation_notes: Comprehensive / thorough — 'X, Y, and everything else'.
Often with contrasting or paired nouns: 手といわず顔といわず, 昼といわず夜といわず.
Literary / emphatic register.
Similar in function to 〜を問わず but more emphatic.

examples.japanese: 昼といわず夜といわず、働き続けた。
examples.english: Day and night alike, he kept working.

examples.japanese: 手といわず顔といわず、泥だらけだった。
examples.english: His hands, his face — everywhere was covered in mud.

examples.japanese: 子供といわず大人といわず、みんな楽しんでいた。
examples.english: Children and adults alike, everyone was enjoying it.

pattern: Noun + であれ + noun + であれ
meaning_en: whether X or Y / no matter if X or Y
meaning_detailed: 〜であれ〜であれ is a formal concessive listing — 'whether it's X or Y, (the main clause holds)'. The main clause holds regardless of which of the listed items is the case. Literary / formal register, common in declarative statements about universal principles.
formation: Noun + であれ + noun + であれ + main clause.
formation_notes: Formal concessive listing — main clause applies to both options.
Literary / formal register.
Similar in function to 〜にしろ〜にしろ (both N2).
Common with contrasting pairs: 男であれ女であれ, 子供であれ大人であれ.

examples.japanese: 男であれ女であれ、誰もが平等だ。
examples.english: Whether man or woman, everyone is equal.

examples.japanese: プロであれアマであれ、基本が大切だ。
examples.english: Whether pro or amateur, the basics are important.

examples.japanese: 大人であれ子供であれ、ルールを守るべきだ。
examples.english: Whether adult or child, one should follow the rules.

pattern: V dict-form + までだ / までのことだ
meaning_en: (I'll) just / I'll simply X (if needed)
meaning_detailed: 〜までだ / 〜までのことだ expresses a speaker's resolve to take a specific (often unremarkable or last-resort) action if necessary — 'I'll just X'. The main clause action is presented as a simple, unchallenging fallback. Formal register, common in declarations of resolve.
formation: V dict-form + までだ / までのことだ.
formation_notes: Resolved fallback — 'in that case, I'll simply X'.
Two variants: までだ (shorter) and までのことだ (slightly more emphatic).
Often follows a hypothetical: だめなら、やめるまでだ ('if it doesn't work, I'll just quit').
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: だめなら、諦めるまでだ。
examples.english: If it doesn't work, I'll just give up.

examples.japanese: できないなら、自分でやるまでだ。
examples.english: If no one can, I'll just do it myself.

examples.japanese: 嫌なら、帰るまでだ。
examples.english: If you don't like it, I'll just go home.

pattern: V-た form + までのことだ
meaning_en: all I did was / it was simply because
meaning_detailed: 〜たまでのことだ downplays an action as unremarkable or modest — 'all I did was X' / 'I was simply doing X'. Common in humble responses to praise or explanations of a small action. Distinct from the V-dict form までだ which expresses future resolve.
formation: V-た + までのことだ.
formation_notes: Modest / downplaying — 'I was just doing X, nothing special'.
Common in humble responses: 礼なんて、当然のことをしたまでのことだ ('you don't need to thank me — I just did what was natural').
Distinct from V dict + までだ (resolve) — this is past-action downplaying.
Formal register.

examples.japanese: お礼を言われることではない。当然のことをしたまでのことです。
examples.english: No need to thank me — I just did what was natural.

examples.japanese: 私は本当のことを言ったまでのことだ。
examples.english: I was only telling the truth.

examples.japanese: 指示に従ったまでのことです。
examples.english: I was just following instructions.

pattern: Plain form + とは
meaning_en: to think that / I didn't expect that / the fact that
meaning_detailed: 〜とは expresses surprise, amazement, or disbelief at a fact — 'to think that X!' / 'I didn't expect that X'. Often used in exclamations of surprise. Can also introduce a definition: '〜とは (the term) X means'. Two related but distinct uses; context disambiguates.
formation: Plain-form clause + とは.
formation_notes: Surprise / disbelief: 彼が来るとは、思わなかった ('I didn't think he'd come').
Definition: 友情とは、助け合うことだ ('friendship means helping each other').
Can stand alone at sentence end: ひどいとは ('how awful').
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: 彼が来るとは思わなかった。
examples.english: I never thought he'd come.

examples.japanese: 友情とは、助け合うことだ。
examples.english: Friendship means helping each other.

examples.japanese: こんなことが起こるとは、信じられない。
examples.english: That something like this would happen — I can't believe it.

pattern: Noun + に至っては
meaning_en: when it comes to / as for (extreme case)
meaning_detailed: 〜に至っては introduces an especially extreme or notable example in a series — 'when it comes to X, (it's even more extreme)'. Often used to single out one case as the most striking or worst among several being discussed. Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + に至っては.
formation_notes: Extreme singling-out — X is the most notable / worst case.
Often follows a list of cases: 〜は、〜は、〜に至っては (a crescendo).
Related: 〜に至る (reach — a separate meaning).
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: 彼の遅刻に至っては、もう許せない。
examples.english: When it comes to his tardiness, I can no longer forgive it.

examples.japanese: 私に至っては、一度も行ったことがない。
examples.english: As for me, I've never even been there.

examples.japanese: 最後の試験に至っては、完全に失敗した。
examples.english: When it came to the last exam, I failed completely.

pattern: Noun + をおいて (+ negative)
meaning_en: other than / except for (no one else but)
meaning_detailed: 〜をおいて introduces a unique / indispensable entity — 'there is no one / nothing other than X' who can do or be Y. Typically paired with a negative main clause for emphasis: 'there's no one but X'. Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + をおいて + negative main clause (ない, いない, etc.).
formation_notes: Exclusivity — X is the only option, no one / nothing else qualifies.
Requires a negative main clause for the 'no one but X' sense.
Formal / literary register — often in praise or assertion of uniqueness.
Close in function to 〜以外 (other than) but with a more emphatic 'only X' flavor.

examples.japanese: 彼をおいて、適任者はいない。
examples.english: There's no one suitable other than him.

examples.japanese: この役は彼女をおいて他にいない。
examples.english: There's no one for this role other than her.

examples.japanese: 今をおいて、他にチャンスはない。
examples.english: There's no chance other than now.

pattern: V dict-form + ことなしに
meaning_en: without doing / without even
meaning_detailed: 〜ことなしに is a formal / literary construction meaning 'without doing X' — the Y clause is achieved or attempted without X having occurred. More formal than 〜ずに or 〜ないで. Often used in declarative statements where X would normally be expected.
formation: V dict-form + ことなしに.
formation_notes: Formal / literary 'without X'.
More formal than 〜ずに (formal) and 〜ないで (neutral).
Common with substantive actions: 努力することなしに, 苦労することなしに.
Formal register.

examples.japanese: 努力することなしに、成功はあり得ない。
examples.english: Without making an effort, success is impossible.

examples.japanese: 苦労することなしに、何も得られない。
examples.english: Without hardship, nothing is gained.

examples.japanese: 彼の助けがあることなしに、できなかった。
examples.english: Without his help, I couldn't have done it.

pattern: Noun (group / category) + きっての
meaning_en: foremost / best in / most distinguished of
meaning_detailed: 〜きっての marks someone or something as the foremost or most distinguished within a group — 'the best of X' / 'foremost in X'. Typically attached to a group noun and followed by an evaluative noun (expert, talent, musician, etc.). Formal / praising register.
formation: Noun (group / category) + きっての + noun (role / quality).
formation_notes: Superlative within a defined group — 'the best of X'.
Common: 会社きっての専門家 ('the company's foremost expert'), 世界きっての音楽家 ('the world's most distinguished musician').
Formal / praising register.
Always followed by an evaluative noun.

examples.japanese: 彼はこの会社きっての専門家だ。
examples.english: He is the foremost expert in this company.

examples.japanese: この町きっての名医を紹介します。
examples.english: I'll introduce the town's most distinguished doctor.

examples.japanese: 彼女は世界きってのピアニストだ。
examples.english: She is the world's most distinguished pianist.

pattern: Noun / V plain + ともなれば
meaning_en: when one becomes / when it comes to being
meaning_detailed: 〜ともなれば expresses 'when one is / becomes X' — emphasizing the natural consequences of reaching or being in a specific role or state. The main clause typically describes the responsibilities or expectations that naturally follow. Formal / literary register. Distinct from 〜となれば (more circumstantial).
formation: Noun / V plain + ともなれば.
formation_notes: Role / state consequences — 'once one is X, then naturally Y'.
Similar to 〜となれば (N3) but more about status / role than circumstance.
Formal / literary register.
Often with adult responsibilities: 大人ともなれば, 社長ともなれば.

examples.japanese: 大人ともなれば、責任を持たなければならない。
examples.english: Once one is an adult, one must bear responsibility.

examples.japanese: 社長ともなれば、多くの判断が求められる。
examples.english: Once one is a company president, many judgments are demanded.

examples.japanese: 親ともなれば、心配が尽きない。
examples.english: Once one is a parent, worries are endless.

pattern: Noun (emotion) + の至り
meaning_en: the height of (an emotion) / extremely
meaning_detailed: 〜の至り expresses the speaker's heightened emotion at its peak — 'the height of X'. Common in humble acknowledgments and formal emotional expressions. Typical nouns: 光栄 ('honor'), 感激 ('deep emotion'), 恥ずかしい (embarrassing), 赤面 (blushing). Very formal / literary register.
formation: Noun (emotion) + の至り.
formation_notes: Extreme emotional expression — 'the height of X'.
Common nouns: 光栄 ('honor'), 感激 ('emotion'), 恐縮, 赤面.
Very formal / humble register — common in speeches and formal letters.
Related: 〜の極み (the ultimate of).

examples.japanese: このような賞をいただき、光栄の至りです。
examples.english: Receiving such an award is the height of honor.

examples.japanese: 初めての出版で、感激の至りです。
examples.english: With my first publication, I'm overwhelmed with emotion.

examples.japanese: お恥ずかしい限りで、赤面の至りです。
examples.english: I'm so embarrassed I could blush.

pattern: V plain / noun + の + かたわら
meaning_en: while also / alongside (doing)
meaning_detailed: 〜かたわら expresses that the subject does a secondary activity alongside the main one — 'while also doing X'. Typically for two long-term ongoing activities (e.g., work and study). Formal / literary register. Different from 〜ながら (simultaneous single-action) in that かたわら is for parallel long-term roles.
formation: V dict-form + かたわら; noun + の + かたわら.
formation_notes: Parallel long-term activities — not simultaneous single actions.
Common: 仕事のかたわら, 勉強のかたわら, 子育てのかたわら.
Formal / literary register.
Distinct from 〜ながら (simultaneous single actions).

examples.japanese: 彼は会社で働くかたわら、夜は大学で学んでいる。
examples.english: He works at a company, while also studying at university at night.

examples.japanese: 子育てのかたわら、趣味を続けている。
examples.english: While raising children, she continues her hobby.

examples.japanese: 仕事のかたわら、ボランティアをしている。
examples.english: Alongside work, I do volunteer activities.

pattern: Verb ます-stem / noun + がてら
meaning_en: while (also) / taking the opportunity to
meaning_detailed: 〜がてら expresses 'while doing X, also do Y (taking the opportunity)'. Similar in function to 〜ついでに but more literary. The main activity and the secondary action are closely tied — Y is a convenient addition to X. Formal register.
formation: V ます-stem + がてら; noun + がてら.
formation_notes: Convenient addition — Y is done taking advantage of X.
Similar to 〜ついでに (more common in speech).
Formal / literary register.
Common with travel / errand: 散歩がてら, 買い物がてら.

examples.japanese: 散歩がてら、買い物に行った。
examples.english: While on a walk, I also went shopping.

examples.japanese: 仕事がてら、観光を楽しんだ。
examples.english: On a work trip, I enjoyed some sightseeing.

examples.japanese: お礼がてら、手土産を持って行った。
examples.english: To express thanks, I brought a gift.

pattern: V dict-form + べからず / べからざる
meaning_en: must not / should not (archaic prohibition)
meaning_detailed: 〜べからず is an archaic / classical prohibition meaning 'must not X'. Rare in modern speech, common in old signs and proverbs. The attributive form べからざる is used as a modifier — 'that must not be Xed'. Fixed form: 〜べからざる+ 行為 ('an act that must not be done').
formation: V dict-form + べからず (sentence-final); V dict-form + べからざる + noun (attributive).
formation_notes: Archaic prohibition — very rare in modern speech.
Common in historical signs: 立ち入るべからず ('no entry').
Attributive べからざる + noun is slightly more alive: 許すべからざる行為 ('an unforgivable act').
Related to 〜べき / 〜べきだ (should) but with negation.

examples.japanese: ここに立ち入るべからず。
examples.english: No entry here.

examples.japanese: これは許すべからざる行為だ。
examples.english: This is an unforgivable act.

examples.japanese: 彼は欠くべからざる存在だ。
examples.english: He is an indispensable presence.

pattern: V negative stem + ずにはすまない / ないではすまない
meaning_en: can't get by without / must inevitably do
meaning_detailed: 〜ずにはすまない expresses that the speaker cannot let X go undone — 'one cannot get by without doing X' / 'X must inevitably happen'. Often used for obligations arising from social or moral pressure. Formal register. Irregular: する → せずにはすまない.
formation: V ない-stem + ずにはすまない. Irregular: する → せずにはすまない.
formation_notes: Social / moral inevitability — one cannot escape doing X.
Irregular: せずにはすまない for する.
Alternative: 〜ないではすまない (slightly more casual).
Formal register.

examples.japanese: 謝らずにはすまないだろう。
examples.english: He'll have no choice but to apologize.

examples.japanese: この問題は解決せずにはすまない。
examples.english: This problem must inevitably be resolved.

examples.japanese: 責任を取らずにはすまない。
examples.english: He cannot get by without taking responsibility.

pattern: Number / noun + といったところだ
meaning_en: about / approximately / around
meaning_detailed: 〜といったところだ expresses an approximate amount, degree, or description — 'about X' / 'roughly X'. Often used to give a ballpark estimate. Similar to 〜ぐらい / 〜ほど but more descriptive and formal. Formal register.
formation: Number / noun / phrase + といったところだ.
formation_notes: Approximate estimate — 'about / roughly X'.
Common with numbers or descriptive terms.
Formal / literary register.
Related to 〜と言える ('can be said to be').

examples.japanese: 参加者は50人といったところだ。
examples.english: The participants are about 50 people.

examples.japanese: 今週末は少し休めるといったところだ。
examples.english: This weekend I can rest a little, roughly speaking.

examples.japanese: 勉強時間は1日2時間といったところです。
examples.english: My study time is roughly 2 hours per day.

pattern: V volitional + にも + V potential negative
meaning_en: can't (do X) even if one wanted to
meaning_detailed: 〜ようにも〜ない expresses that an action is impossible even though the subject would like to do it — 'I can't do X even if I wanted to'. The X verb appears twice: once in volitional form + にも, then in potential negative. Literary / formal register.
formation: V volitional + にも + same V potential negative. E.g., 行こうにも行けない, 言おうにも言えない.
formation_notes: Double-verb structure — the same verb appears in volitional + にも and potential negative.
Literary register — common in narratives of frustrated action.
Close to 〜たくても〜ない (casual 'want to but can't').
Formal register.

examples.japanese: 言おうにも言えない状況だ。
examples.english: It's a situation where I can't speak even if I wanted to.

examples.japanese: 行こうにも足が痛くて行けない。
examples.english: Even if I wanted to go, my leg hurts so I can't.

examples.japanese: 忘れようにも忘れられない。
examples.english: Even if I wanted to forget, I can't.

pattern: Verb dict-form + んがため(に)
meaning_en: in order to / for the purpose of (literary)
meaning_detailed: 〜んがため(に) is a literary / archaic purpose marker meaning 'in order to X'. More formal and older-sounding than 〜ために or 〜ように. Rare in modern speech, common in essays, historical writing, and formal prose. Irregular: する → せんがため.
formation: V dict-form (without final る for ichidan) + んがため(に). Irregular: する → せんがため.
formation_notes: Very literary / archaic purpose marker.
Irregular: せんがため for する.
Rare in casual speech — common in literature, essays, and formal prose.
More emphatic and older-sounding than 〜ために.

examples.japanese: 真実を知らんがために、調査を続けた。
examples.english: In order to know the truth, he continued the investigation.

examples.japanese: 夢を実現せんがため、努力している。
examples.english: For the purpose of realizing his dream, he is working hard.

examples.japanese: 勝利を得んがために、全力を尽くした。
examples.english: In order to gain victory, he gave his all.

pattern: Verb dict / V-た + んばかり(に)
meaning_en: as if about to / looking as if
meaning_detailed: 〜んばかり(に) expresses that something looks as if it is about to happen or happen intensely — 'as if about to X'. Often used metaphorically for emphasis: 泣かんばかりに ('as if about to cry'). Literary register. The ん is from archaic negative む, but the meaning is 'about to'.
formation: V dict (without final る) + んばかり(に) / んばかりだ.
formation_notes: Metaphorical 'as if about to' — often hyperbolic.
Common: 泣かんばかりに, 飛びかからんばかりに, 叫ばんばかりに.
Literary register — rare in casual speech.
The ん is an archaic form; the meaning here is not negation.

examples.japanese: 彼女は泣かんばかりの表情だった。
examples.english: She had an expression as if she were about to cry.

examples.japanese: 彼は飛び上がらんばかりに喜んだ。
examples.english: He rejoiced as if about to jump for joy.

examples.japanese: 叫ばんばかりに抗議した。
examples.english: He protested as if about to scream.

pattern: Verb volitional + にも
meaning_en: even if one tried to / even attempting to
meaning_detailed: Verb volitional + にも is part of the 〜ようにも〜ない construction (covered separately as you-ni-mo-nai). This entry captures the standalone 'even if one tries to X' meaning, often found in impossibility statements. Literary register.
formation: V volitional + にも + (main clause expressing impossibility).
formation_notes: Typically part of 〜ようにも〜ない ('can't even if one tried').
Expresses attempted but failed / impossible action.
Literary register.
Very similar in structure to 〜ようと as literary concessive.

examples.japanese: 寝ようにも、眠れない。
examples.english: Even if I try to sleep, I can't sleep.

examples.japanese: 帰ろうにも、終電が終わってしまった。
examples.english: Even if I wanted to go home, the last train is gone.

examples.japanese: 説明しようにも、言葉が見つからない。
examples.english: Even if I try to explain, I can't find the words.

pattern: Verb volitional + ものなら
meaning_en: if one were to X / if you dare to X
meaning_detailed: Verb volitional + ものなら expresses a hypothetical with a negative consequence — 'if you were to do X (dare to do X), something bad would happen'. Often a warning or threat. Distinct from 〜ものなら with potential form (if it were possible — covered at N3 mono-nara-if-possible).
formation: V volitional (よう / おう) + ものなら.
formation_notes: Hypothetical with warning / threat — 'if you dare X, Y will happen'.
Often negative consequences.
Distinct from potential form + ものなら (if it were possible).
Literary / formal register.

examples.japanese: 遅刻しようものなら、上司に叱られる。
examples.english: If you dare to be late, the boss will scold you.

examples.japanese: 文句を言おうものなら、すぐ怒り出す。
examples.english: If you complain, he'll get angry right away.

examples.japanese: 失敗しようものなら、大変なことになる。
examples.english: If you fail, it'll be a disaster.

pattern: Number + counter + たりとも + negative
meaning_en: not even one / not even a single
meaning_detailed: 〜たりとも attached to a minimal number (一 'one') emphasizes that not even the smallest unit is acceptable / available — 'not even one X'. Always followed by a negative main clause. Literary / formal register, common in strict declarations.
formation: Number + counter + たりとも + negative. Often 一 + counter + たりとも.
formation_notes: Emphatic minimum denial — 'not even one'.
Always with a negative main clause.
Formal / literary register.
Common fixed form: 一瞬たりとも ('not even for a moment'), 一円たりとも ('not even one yen').

examples.japanese: 一瞬たりとも油断できない。
examples.english: I can't let my guard down for even a moment.

examples.japanese: 一円たりとも無駄にしない。
examples.english: I won't waste even a single yen.

examples.japanese: 一度たりとも彼を疑ったことはない。
examples.english: I've never doubted him, not even once.

pattern: Noun + なしに(は)
meaning_en: without / unless
meaning_detailed: 〜なしに expresses 'without X' — the action is done or the state exists in the absence of X. Similar to 〜ないで / 〜ずに but attached to nouns. The longer なしには adds emphasis. Often paired with a negative main clause for 'cannot without X' sense. Formal register.
formation: Noun + なしに(は).
formation_notes: Noun-based 'without' — similar to ないで but attached to nouns.
なしには (with は) emphasizes the absence.
Often in impossibility statements: 〜なしには成功できない.
Formal register.

examples.japanese: 許可なしに、ここに入ってはいけない。
examples.english: You must not enter here without permission.

examples.japanese: 努力なしには、成功できない。
examples.english: Without effort, you cannot succeed.

examples.japanese: 彼女の助けなしに、やり遂げられなかった。
examples.english: Without her help, I couldn't have completed it.

pattern: Noun + なくして(は)
meaning_en: without / unable to X without
meaning_detailed: 〜なくしては is a formal / literary 'without' construction — the main-clause action is impossible without X. Very close in meaning to 〜なしには but more literary / emphatic. Often in declarations about essential conditions for success, happiness, etc. Formal register.
formation: Noun + なくして(は) + negative / impossibility main clause.
formation_notes: Very formal / literary 'without X'.
Similar to 〜なしには — slight stylistic difference.
Typically in declarative statements about essential conditions.
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: 愛なくして、人生は語れない。
examples.english: Life cannot be spoken of without love.

examples.japanese: 彼の協力なくしては、成功は不可能だった。
examples.english: Without his cooperation, success would have been impossible.

examples.japanese: 努力なくして、目標は達成できない。
examples.english: Without effort, goals cannot be achieved.

pattern: Verb dict-form + や / や否や
meaning_en: at the moment of / as soon as (literary)
meaning_detailed: 〜や / 〜や否や is a literary / classical 'as soon as' construction. Very dramatic in tone, common in classical prose and formal narratives. Similar in meaning to 〜なり (N2 literary 'as soon as') and 〜たとたん (N3). Verb stays in dictionary form.
formation: V dict-form + や / や否や.
formation_notes: Literary / classical 'as soon as'.
や alone or や否や (same meaning).
More dramatic than modern equivalents (たとたん, なり, や否や is N2 version).
Very formal register — rare in casual speech.

examples.japanese: 彼は家を出るや、走り出した。
examples.english: The moment he left the house, he ran off.

examples.japanese: 目を開けるや否や、眩しい光が差し込んだ。
examples.english: The moment I opened my eyes, bright light poured in.

examples.japanese: 話を聞くや、彼は立ち上がった。
examples.english: The moment he heard the story, he stood up.

pattern: Noun + の極み
meaning_en: the height of / the ultimate / extreme
meaning_detailed: 〜の極み expresses an extreme degree of a quality, emotion, or state — 'the height of X' / 'the ultimate X'. Similar to 〜の至り but with a slightly more physical / descriptive feel. Common nouns: 贅沢 ('luxury'), 感動 ('emotion'), 疲労 ('fatigue'), 幸せ ('happiness'). Very formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + の極み.
formation_notes: Extreme degree — 'the ultimate / peak of X'.
Common: 贅沢の極み, 疲労の極み, 感動の極み, 幸せの極み.
Very formal / literary register — common in praise and emphatic descriptions.
Related: 〜の至り (more emotional / humble).

examples.japanese: このホテルは贅沢の極みだ。
examples.english: This hotel is the height of luxury.

examples.japanese: 疲労の極みに達した。
examples.english: I reached the extreme of fatigue.

examples.japanese: 子供の笑顔は幸せの極みだ。
examples.english: A child's smile is the ultimate happiness.

pattern: V plain / noun + の + 手前
meaning_en: in view of / out of consideration for / for the sake of appearance
meaning_detailed: 〜手前 expresses that an action is taken out of consideration for something or someone — often 'out of face' or 'in view of X'. The speaker feels obliged to act a certain way because of social appearance or previous commitments. Formal / literary register.
formation: V plain + 手前; noun + の + 手前.
formation_notes: Social / face-saving consideration — 'out of consideration for X'.
Often when the speaker must act a certain way for appearances.
手前 literally means 'in front / before' — here metaphorically 'in front of others' view'.
Formal register.

examples.japanese: 約束した手前、引き下がれない。
examples.english: Having promised, I can't back down.

examples.japanese: 子供の手前、ちゃんとしなければならない。
examples.english: For the sake of the children, I have to behave properly.

examples.japanese: みんなの手前、断れなかった。
examples.english: In front of everyone, I couldn't refuse.

pattern: Noun + に則って / に則り
meaning_en: in accordance with / according to (rules, customs)
meaning_detailed: 〜に則って expresses that an action conforms to or follows established rules, customs, precedents, or principles — 'in accordance with X'. More formal than 〜に従って. Common in legal, traditional, and ceremonial contexts. The verb 則る literally means 'to conform to'.
formation: Noun + に則って / に則り.
formation_notes: Formal conformity — 'in accordance with rules / tradition'.
Common with 法律, 規則, 伝統, 慣習, 方針.
More formal than 〜に従って.
Very formal — common in legal and ceremonial contexts.

examples.japanese: 伝統に則って、式を行った。
examples.english: We held the ceremony in accordance with tradition.

examples.japanese: 法律に則り、処理した。
examples.english: I handled it in accordance with the law.

examples.japanese: 規則に則って行動してください。
examples.english: Please act in accordance with the rules.

pattern: Noun + をもって
meaning_en: by means of / with / as of (formal)
meaning_detailed: 〜をもって has several related senses: (1) 'by means of' — formal equivalent of で; (2) 'as of / with (this)' — marking a transition point or deadline; (3) with a singular instance implying 'by this one X, (Y is achieved)'. Very formal / literary register, common in official announcements and ceremonial language.
formation: Noun + をもって.
formation_notes: Very formal — 'by means of / with'.
Time / deadline sense: 本日をもって ('as of today').
Means / method sense: 努力をもって ('by means of effort').
Often in formal closing announcements.

examples.japanese: これをもって、会議を終わります。
examples.english: With this, we conclude the meeting.

examples.japanese: 努力をもって、成功を勝ち取った。
examples.english: By means of effort, he achieved success.

examples.japanese: 本日をもって、退職いたします。
examples.english: As of today, I am retiring.

pattern: V plain / adj + ところを
meaning_en: (although) at / while / in the middle of (polite apology)
meaning_detailed: 〜ところを is a polite / formal construction used when interrupting or apologizing for taking someone's time — 'while X (I apologize for disturbing)'. Common in business and formal speech, especially in the phrase お忙しいところを ('while you're busy'). Formal register.
formation: V plain / i-adj + ところを; na-adj + な + ところを; noun + の + ところを.
formation_notes: Polite apology for interrupting — formal business register.
Common fixed phrase: お忙しいところを (apologizing for taking busy time).
The main clause typically expresses the action of taking up someone's time.
Formal / polite register.

examples.japanese: お忙しいところを、ありがとうございます。
examples.english: Thank you for your time despite being busy.

examples.japanese: お休みのところをすみません。
examples.english: I'm sorry to disturb your rest.

examples.japanese: お食事中のところを失礼します。
examples.english: Excuse me for interrupting your meal.

pattern: V-て form + はばからない
meaning_en: does not hesitate to / declares openly
meaning_detailed: 〜てはばからない expresses that the subject does something without hesitation or inhibition — 'does X openly / unapologetically'. Often with assertions, declarations, or bold actions. Usually with a note of surprise or criticism from the speaker at the directness. Literary / formal register.
formation: V-て + はばからない.
formation_notes: 'Doesn't hold back' — often implies directness that surprises or criticizes.
Common with verbs of speaking: 言ってはばからない, 主張してはばからない.
Literary / formal register.
The verb はばかる literally means 'to hesitate / fear'.

examples.japanese: 彼は自分が一番だと言ってはばからない。
examples.english: He openly declares he's the best.

examples.japanese: その政治家は改革を主張してはばからなかった。
examples.english: The politician unhesitatingly advocated for reform.

examples.japanese: 彼女は意見を言ってはばからない性格だ。
examples.english: She has a personality that doesn't hesitate to voice opinions.

pattern: Verb dict-form + べくもない
meaning_en: no way to / cannot possibly
meaning_detailed: 〜べくもない expresses that something is impossible — 'there is no way to X'. Built on the classical べし ('should / must') with negation. More literary / emphatic than 〜ようがない. Rare in modern casual speech, common in literary and formal writing.
formation: V dict-form + べくもない.
formation_notes: Literary 'no way to / cannot possibly'.
Related to 〜べき / 〜べからず via the classical べし.
More formal than 〜ようがない.
Common in literature and formal prose.

examples.japanese: 彼の意図を知るべくもない。
examples.english: There's no way to know his intentions.

examples.japanese: 結果を予測すべくもない。
examples.english: There's no way to predict the result.

examples.japanese: 真実を知るべくもなかった。
examples.english: There was no way to know the truth.

pattern: Noun / plain form + とやら
meaning_en: some kind of / (I hear) something like
meaning_detailed: 〜とやら expresses that the speaker is uncertain about the exact detail — 'some kind of X' / 'something called X'. The speaker has heard of X but is hazy on specifics. Common in conversation when referring to things secondhand. Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + とやら; plain-form clause + とやら.
formation_notes: Vagueness / secondhand knowledge — the speaker doesn't fully know.
Common when referring to things by name without commitment: 田中さんとやら ('someone named Tanaka').
Formal / literary register.
Can also introduce hearsay: 〜だとやら ('I hear that it's...').

examples.japanese: 田中さんとやらは、どこの人ですか。
examples.english: This Tanaka person — where is he from?

examples.japanese: 何とやらいう病気にかかったらしい。
examples.english: It seems he caught some kind of illness.

examples.japanese: 彼は新しい会社を作ったとやら。
examples.english: I hear he started a new company or something.

pattern: V ない-form + でもない
meaning_en: not that I don't / it's not impossible to
meaning_detailed: 〜ないでもない is a double negative that expresses qualified agreement or capability — 'it's not that I don't X' / 'I could X (somewhat)'. Similar in meaning to 〜ないことはない but slightly more casual. Hedges a positive claim by denying the negative.
formation: V ない-form + でもない.
formation_notes: Double negative — softens a positive claim.
Similar to 〜ないことはない (N3) and 〜なくもない (N2).
Conversational register.
Often expresses reluctant or qualified agreement.

examples.japanese: 行かないでもないが、気が進まない。
examples.english: It's not that I won't go, but I'm not enthusiastic.

examples.japanese: 分からないでもない。
examples.english: It's not that I don't understand.

examples.japanese: できないでもないけど、時間がかかる。
examples.english: It's not impossible, but it would take time.

pattern: Noun + を皮切りに(して)
meaning_en: starting with / beginning with
meaning_detailed: 〜を皮切りに expresses that something is the first in a series — 'starting with X, (then Y, Z followed)'. Common in descriptions of events, tours, or sequences where X is the starting point. Formal register, common in news and business contexts.
formation: Noun + を皮切りに / を皮切りにして.
formation_notes: Series starter — X is the first of multiple things.
Common for tours, launches, series: 東京を皮切りに, 初日を皮切りに.
Attributive form を皮切りとした + noun.
Formal / news register.

examples.japanese: 東京を皮切りに、全国ツアーが始まる。
examples.english: Starting with Tokyo, the national tour begins.

examples.japanese: 彼の発言を皮切りに、議論が始まった。
examples.english: Starting with his remark, the discussion began.

examples.japanese: 初日を皮切りに、多くの観客が訪れた。
examples.english: Starting on the first day, many spectators visited.

pattern: Noun + を限りに
meaning_en: with X being the last / as of X (final)
meaning_detailed: 〜を限りに expresses a final endpoint — 'with X being the last' / 'as of X (and no more after)'. Often used for retirements, final appearances, cessations of activity. Formal register, common in announcements of endings. Distinct from 〜限り (as long as / scope).
formation: Noun + を限りに. Often with time / event nouns.
formation_notes: Final endpoint — X is the last moment / instance.
Common for retirements / closings: 本日を限りに ('as of today'), 今年を限りに.
Distinct from 〜限り (scope / limit).
Formal / announcement register.

examples.japanese: 今月を限りに、この店は閉店します。
examples.english: As of this month, this store will close.

examples.japanese: 今日を限りに、タバコをやめる。
examples.english: As of today, I'll quit smoking.

examples.japanese: 今年を限りに引退する。
examples.english: I'll retire as of this year.

pattern: V plain / noun + の + 折(に)
meaning_en: on the occasion of / when
meaning_detailed: 〜折(に) is a formal / polite temporal marker meaning 'on the occasion of' — a specific moment or opportunity. More formal than 〜とき, often used in polite correspondence and formal speech. Common in set phrases and polite framings.
formation: V plain + 折(に); noun + の + 折(に).
formation_notes: Formal 'on the occasion of' — more polite than とき or 際.
Common in polite correspondence: お近くにお越しの折には ('when you come near us').
Often implies a specific, notable opportunity.
Formal / polite register.

examples.japanese: お近くにお越しの折には、お寄りください。
examples.english: When you come near us, please drop by.

examples.japanese: 結婚式の折には、彼も出席した。
examples.english: On the occasion of the wedding, he also attended.

examples.japanese: ご入り用の折には、ご連絡ください。
examples.english: When you need it, please contact us.

pattern: Noun / V stem + ながらに(して)
meaning_en: while / in (the state of) / from the start
meaning_detailed: 〜ながらに is a literary construction expressing that the main action is done 'while in the state of X' or 'in a way that preserves X'. Often with 生まれ (born), 涙 (tears), 昔 (the past): 生まれながらに ('since birth'), 涙ながらに ('tearfully'), 昔ながらに ('as in the old days'). Distinct from 〜ながら (simultaneous single-action).
formation: Noun / V ます-stem + ながらに(して).
formation_notes: Literary 'while in / from' — state-preserving.
Common fixed forms: 生まれながらに, 涙ながらに, 昔ながらに, 居ながらにして.
Distinct from 〜ながら (simultaneous action).
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: 彼は生まれながらにして、才能があった。
examples.english: He had talent from birth.

examples.japanese: 彼女は涙ながらに語った。
examples.english: She spoke through tears.

examples.japanese: 昔ながらの味が楽しめる。
examples.english: You can enjoy the traditional flavor.

pattern: Verb ます-stem + おおせる
meaning_en: succeed in completing / manage to do fully
meaning_detailed: 〜おおせる is a literary auxiliary meaning 'succeed in completing X' — the subject managed to do X fully, often despite difficulty or being under pressure. Carries a note of accomplishment. Common with verbs of accomplishment, deception, and task completion. Literary register.
formation: V ます-stem + おおせる.
formation_notes: Literary auxiliary — 'succeed in fully doing X'.
Often with difficult or secretive actions: 逃げおおせる ('succeed in escaping'), 言いおおせる ('manage to say'), やりおおせる ('manage to accomplish').
Literary / formal register.
Related to 終える (finish) but emphasizes accomplishment.

examples.japanese: 犯人は警察から逃げおおせた。
examples.english: The criminal succeeded in escaping the police.

examples.japanese: 何とかやりおおせた。
examples.english: Somehow I managed to accomplish it.

examples.japanese: 嘘をつきおおせるのは難しい。
examples.english: It's hard to successfully maintain a lie.

pattern: Verb ます-stem + こなす
meaning_en: handle skillfully / master / do expertly
meaning_detailed: 〜こなす is an auxiliary expressing that the subject handles an action skillfully or masterfully — 'do X expertly'. Often with language, technology, or complex tasks: 使いこなす ('master the use of'), 乗りこなす ('ride skillfully'), 着こなす ('wear stylishly'). Neutral / formal register.
formation: V ます-stem + こなす.
formation_notes: Skillful mastery — 'handle X expertly'.
Common: 使いこなす (master using), 乗りこなす (ride well), 着こなす (wear stylishly).
The independent verb こなす means 'to handle / digest'.
Neutral / formal register.

examples.japanese: 彼は英語を使いこなしている。
examples.english: He has mastered the use of English.

examples.japanese: 新しいソフトを使いこなすのに時間がかかった。
examples.english: It took time to master the new software.

examples.japanese: 彼女は着物を美しく着こなしている。
examples.english: She wears kimono beautifully.

pattern: Noun + めかす
meaning_en: hint at / show a look of
meaning_detailed: 〜めかす is a literary suffix that creates verbs meaning 'to show a hint of X' or 'to act as if X'. Common with emotional or attitude nouns: 冗談めかす ('hint jokingly'), 秘密めかす ('hint mysteriously / act secretive'). Related to the suffix 〜めく (have the look of) but active-transitive — the subject projects the quality.
formation: Noun + めかす (conjugates as godan u-verb).
formation_notes: Active suffix — subject projects / shows the quality.
Related to 〜めく (passive 'have the look of') — めかす is active.
Common: 冗談めかす, 秘密めかす, 気取る系なもの.
Literary register.

examples.japanese: 彼は冗談めかして話した。
examples.english: He spoke with a joking air.

examples.japanese: 彼女は秘密めかして微笑んだ。
examples.english: She smiled with a mysterious air.

examples.japanese: 皮肉めかした言い方をやめてほしい。
examples.english: I wish he'd stop speaking with that ironic air.

pattern: Noun + に他ならない
meaning_en: is nothing but / is exactly / is none other than
meaning_detailed: 〜に他ならない expresses strong identification — 'X is nothing but Y' / 'X is exactly Y, and nothing else'. Used for emphatic definitions or affirmations. Formal / literary register, common in essays and declarative statements.
formation: Noun + に他ならない.
formation_notes: Emphatic identification — 'X is precisely Y, no other'.
Formal / literary register.
Common in essays and opinion pieces.
Related to the everyday 〜にすぎない (nothing more than) but affirmative rather than dismissive.

examples.japanese: これは努力の結果に他ならない。
examples.english: This is nothing but the result of effort.

examples.japanese: 成功は運ではなく、準備に他ならない。
examples.english: Success is not luck but preparation, nothing else.

examples.japanese: この問題の原因は無関心に他ならない。
examples.english: The cause of this problem is nothing but indifference.

pattern: Noun + もさることながら
meaning_en: not to mention / while X is also (true)
meaning_detailed: 〜もさることながら presents X as true / important while pointing out that Y is even more important or notable — 'while X is also the case, Y is especially so'. Formal / literary register, common in reviews, analyses, and balanced descriptions.
formation: Noun + もさることながら.
formation_notes: 'Not only X but especially Y' — formal balanced comparison.
More literary / elevated than 〜だけでなく or 〜ばかりでなく.
Common in reviews: 味もさることながら、雰囲気もいい ('the flavor is good, and especially the atmosphere').
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: 味もさることながら、雰囲気も素晴らしい。
examples.english: The flavor is excellent, and so is the atmosphere.

examples.japanese: 実力もさることながら、努力も素晴らしい。
examples.english: His ability is notable, and so is his effort.

examples.japanese: 値段もさることながら、品質も気になる。
examples.english: The price matters, but so does the quality.

pattern: V dict-form + までもなく
meaning_en: without even needing to / not even requiring
meaning_detailed: 〜までもなく expresses that something was achieved or concluded without needing to go so far as to do X — 'without even needing X'. Often a shortened form of 〜までもない ('no need to even'). Used to show that a result was reached easily or that a step was unnecessary. Formal register.
formation: V dict-form + までもなく.
formation_notes: Related to 〜までもない (no need to even).
The adverbial form until (-naku) modifies the main clause — 'without even requiring X'.
Common fixed form: 言うまでもなく ('needless to say').
Formal / neutral register.

examples.japanese: 言うまでもなく、彼は天才だ。
examples.english: Needless to say, he is a genius.

examples.japanese: 調べるまでもなく、答えは明らかだ。
examples.english: Without even needing to check, the answer is obvious.

examples.japanese: 聞くまでもなく、理由は分かっている。
examples.english: Without even needing to ask, I know the reason.

pattern: Noun / plain form + ならまだしも
meaning_en: if it were at least X (that would be OK, but)
meaning_detailed: 〜ならまだしも expresses that while X would be acceptable or tolerable, the actual situation (Y, described in the main clause) goes beyond what is OK — 'if only X, that would be fine, but Y is too much'. Used to escalate criticism or complaint by comparison.
formation: Noun + ならまだしも; plain-form clause + ならまだしも.
formation_notes: Escalating criticism — 'X would be OK, but Y crosses the line'.
Common in complaints and disappointment: 一度ならまだしも、何度もは困る ('once would be OK, but many times is a problem').
Formal / literary register.
Main clause usually expresses that the reality is worse than X.

examples.japanese: 一度ならまだしも、何度も遅刻するのは困る。
examples.english: Once I could accept, but being late repeatedly is a problem.

examples.japanese: 無料ならまだしも、有料でこれはひどい。
examples.english: If it were free it'd be OK, but for pay, this is awful.

examples.japanese: 子供ならまだしも、大人がそんなことをするとは。
examples.english: If it were a child it would be understandable, but for an adult to do that!

pattern: Noun / plain form + ならいざ知らず
meaning_en: if it were X, I wouldn't know / X might be one thing but
meaning_detailed: 〜ならいざ知らず expresses that while X might be a legitimate exception, Y (the main clause situation) is definitely not — 'X might be understandable, but Y isn't'. Similar in function to 〜ならまだしも but with a slightly more dismissive / exceptional framing. Literary / formal register.
formation: Noun / plain form + ならいざ知らず.
formation_notes: Contrastive exception — 'X might be a case where I couldn't say, but Y is clearly not'.
Similar to 〜ならまだしも but slightly more dismissive.
Literary / formal register.
Main clause typically expresses surprise or criticism.

examples.japanese: 子供ならいざ知らず、大人がそんな失敗をするなんて。
examples.english: If it were a child I wouldn't know, but for an adult to make such a mistake!

examples.japanese: 初心者ならいざ知らず、プロがミスするとは。
examples.english: For a beginner, maybe — but for a pro to make a mistake!

examples.japanese: 昔ならいざ知らず、今の時代に合わない。
examples.english: For olden times maybe, but it doesn't fit this era.

pattern: ひとたび + V + (ば / と / たら)
meaning_en: once (X happens)
meaning_detailed: 〜ひとたび expresses 'once X happens, (Y inevitably follows)'. The 一たび literally means 'one time', and the construction emphasizes that even a single instance of X is enough to trigger Y. Formal / literary register, often in warnings or declarations of inevitability.
formation: ひとたび + V plain + ば / と / たら + main clause.
formation_notes: 'Once X, Y inevitably' — emphasizes that one instance is sufficient.
Often followed by ば, と, or たら.
Literary / formal register.
The kanji form 一度 or 一たび is also possible.

examples.japanese: ひとたび決めたら、最後までやり遂げる。
examples.english: Once I've decided, I see it through to the end.

examples.japanese: ひとたび約束したら、守るべきだ。
examples.english: Once you've promised, you should keep it.

examples.japanese: ひとたび火がつくと、消すのが大変だ。
examples.english: Once a fire starts, it's hard to put out.

pattern: Plain form + としたところで
meaning_en: even if we assume / even for
meaning_detailed: 〜としたところで is a hypothetical concessive — 'even if we assume X, Y'. Similar to 〜にしたところで and 〜としても, hedging a hypothetical and presenting a main-clause claim that holds regardless. Formal / literary register.
formation: Plain-form clause + としたところで.
formation_notes: Formal hypothetical concession.
Similar to 〜にしたところで and 〜としても.
Common in analytical reasoning and argumentation.
Formal register.

examples.japanese: 仮に成功したとしたところで、喜べない。
examples.english: Even if we assume it succeeded, I can't be happy.

examples.japanese: 行ったとしたところで、何も変わらない。
examples.english: Even assuming I went, nothing would change.

examples.japanese: 彼が謝ったとしたところで、許せない。
examples.english: Even if he apologized, I couldn't forgive.

pattern: Noun / plain form + ほどの + noun
meaning_en: of such a degree / to the extent of (a)
meaning_detailed: 〜ほどの + noun expresses that the following noun has the degree or extent of X — 'a noun of the extent / caliber of X'. Used to describe high-degree qualities or notable examples. Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun / V plain + ほどの + noun.
formation_notes: Extent modifier — describes a noun with the degree of X.
Common: 涙が出るほどの感動 ('emotion to the extent of tears'), これほどの大事件 ('an incident of this magnitude').
Formal / literary register.
Related to 〜ほど (extent — N3).

examples.japanese: これほどの大事件は初めてだ。
examples.english: An incident of this magnitude is a first.

examples.japanese: 涙が出るほどの感動だった。
examples.english: It was emotion to the extent of tears.

examples.japanese: 彼は奇跡と呼べるほどの回復を見せた。
examples.english: He showed a recovery you could call a miracle.

pattern: V negative stem + ずもがな
meaning_en: unnecessary / better left unsaid
meaning_detailed: 〜ずもがな is a literary construction meaning 'unnecessary' or 'better left undone' — what's described is better not done. Often with verbs of speaking: 言わずもがな ('needless to say / better left unsaid'). Very literary, almost fixed-phrase register.
formation: V ない-stem + ずもがな. Most common: 言わずもがな.
formation_notes: Literary / classical — 'better left undone / unsaid'.
Almost exclusively in fixed phrases, most commonly 言わずもがな.
Very rare outside these fixed forms.
Classical / literary register.

examples.japanese: 言わずもがなのことを言ってしまった。
examples.english: I said something that would have been better left unsaid.

examples.japanese: 言わずもがな、健康が一番大切だ。
examples.english: Needless to say, health is most important.

examples.japanese: それは言わずもがなのことだ。
examples.english: That goes without saying.

pattern: Noun + にまつわる
meaning_en: related to / concerning / surrounding
meaning_detailed: 〜にまつわる is a formal / literary modifier meaning 'related to / concerning'. Attaches to a noun to describe things connected with X. Common with stories, legends, controversies, memories. The verb まつわる means 'to cling to / be connected with'. Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + にまつわる + noun (attributive); noun + にまつわり (adverbial).
formation_notes: Formal / literary 'connected with / concerning'.
Common: 彼にまつわる噂 ('rumors surrounding him'), 歴史にまつわる話 ('stories related to history').
Related to 〜に関する (regarding) but with a more 'surrounding / entangled' feel.
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: 彼にまつわる噂を聞いた。
examples.english: I heard rumors surrounding him.

examples.japanese: この城にまつわる伝説がある。
examples.english: There's a legend related to this castle.

examples.japanese: お金にまつわる問題が多い。
examples.english: There are many problems related to money.

pattern: Noun + を兼ねて
meaning_en: serving (as both) / combined with
meaning_detailed: 〜を兼ねて expresses that an action or object serves a dual purpose — 'serving (both) as X'. Often used when one activity accomplishes multiple goals: 挨拶を兼ねて ('serving also as a greeting'), 観光を兼ねて ('also functioning as sightseeing'). Formal / neutral register.
formation: Noun + を兼ねて; attributive: 〜を兼ねた + noun.
formation_notes: Dual purpose — the action serves two roles.
Common: 挨拶を兼ねて, 散歩を兼ねて, 運動を兼ねて.
Formal / neutral register.
The verb 兼ねる means 'to combine / serve both'.

examples.japanese: 散歩を兼ねて、買い物に行った。
examples.english: I went shopping, also taking it as a walk.

examples.japanese: 挨拶を兼ねて、お土産を持っていった。
examples.english: I brought a gift, also serving as a greeting.

examples.japanese: 観光を兼ねた出張だった。
examples.english: It was a business trip that also served as sightseeing.

pattern: Noun / na-adj + でありながら
meaning_en: while being / even though one is
meaning_detailed: 〜でありながら is a formal concessive meaning 'while being X, (nonetheless Y)'. Emphasizes a contrast between X (an identity or state) and Y (an unexpected continuation). Literary / formal register, common in essays and balanced analysis.
formation: Noun / na-adj root + でありながら.
formation_notes: Formal / literary concessive — 'while being X, yet Y'.
Often with roles or identities: 子供でありながら, 医者でありながら.
Compare with 〜ながら (simultaneity) — でありながら is specifically for state/identity contrast.
Formal register.

examples.japanese: 子供でありながら、大人のように話す。
examples.english: Even though he's a child, he talks like an adult.

examples.japanese: プロでありながら、基本的なミスをした。
examples.english: Even though he's a pro, he made a basic mistake.

examples.japanese: 医者でありながら、自分の健康を気にしない。
examples.english: Although he's a doctor, he doesn't care about his own health.

pattern: V-て form + みせる
meaning_en: will definitely do / I'll show you (determination)
meaning_detailed: 〜てみせる expresses the speaker's determination to accomplish something — 'I'll definitely do X (and show you I can)'. Different from 〜てみる (try doing) in that みせる literally means 'to show', expressing pride or defiance about proving capability. Used in dramatic or resolute declarations.
formation: V-て + みせる.
formation_notes: Determined declaration — 'I'll show you / prove I can'.
Often in challenging contexts: 絶対に合格してみせる ('I'll definitely pass, you'll see').
Distinct from 〜てみる (try doing).
Dramatic / emphatic register — common in resolute speech.

examples.japanese: 絶対に合格してみせる。
examples.english: I'll definitely pass, you'll see.

examples.japanese: 必ず成功してみせます。
examples.english: I will definitely succeed.

examples.japanese: この困難を乗り越えてみせる。
examples.english: I'll overcome this difficulty, just watch.

pattern: Noun + にして初めて
meaning_en: only at / not until one is (X) / for someone of X's level
meaning_detailed: 〜にして初めて expresses that something is possible or happens 'only upon (reaching / being) X' — often a significant level, role, or position. Related to 〜て初めて (N3, 'only after doing') but with a noun base. Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + にして初めて + main clause.
formation_notes: 'Only when one is / reaches X' — emphasizing an exclusive threshold.
Common with ages, positions, achievements.
Related to 〜て初めて (N3) but attached to a noun.
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: 30歳にして初めて、親の苦労が分かった。
examples.english: Only at 30 did I understand my parents' hardship.

examples.japanese: 彼にして初めてできる技だ。
examples.english: It's a technique only someone of his level can do.

examples.japanese: プロにして初めて、これが分かる。
examples.english: Only as a pro do you understand this.

pattern: Noun + ながらの + noun
meaning_en: in its original state of / traditional / just like
meaning_detailed: 〜ながらの is the attributive form of 〜ながらに, meaning 'in the original state of X' or 'just like X'. Common for describing traditional or preserved qualities: 昔ながらの町 ('a town just as it was in the old days'), 生まれながらの才能 ('innate talent'). Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + ながらの + noun.
formation_notes: Attributive form of 〜ながらに — 'in the state of X'.
Common for traditional / original / preserved: 昔ながらの, 生まれながらの.
Distinct from 〜ながら (simultaneous action).
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: この店は昔ながらの味を守っている。
examples.english: This shop has preserved its traditional flavor.

examples.japanese: 彼女には生まれながらの才能がある。
examples.english: She has innate, natural talent.

examples.japanese: 昔ながらの風景が残っている。
examples.english: The traditional scenery remains.

pattern: いかんせん (sentence-initial / mid-sentence)
meaning_en: unfortunately / regrettably / the trouble is
meaning_detailed: いかんせん is a literary adverb / connective meaning 'unfortunately' or 'regrettably, the problem is'. Used to introduce a limiting or disappointing fact that the speaker can do nothing about. Literary register, common in essays and reflective writing.
formation: いかんせん, [sentence].
formation_notes: Literary / reflective — 'unfortunately / the trouble is'.
Often followed by a limitation or disappointment: いかんせん、時間がない ('unfortunately, I have no time').
Literary register — rare in casual speech.
Related to いかん (how / depending on).

examples.japanese: やりたいが、いかんせん、時間がない。
examples.english: I want to, but unfortunately, I have no time.

examples.japanese: いかんせん、予算が足りない。
examples.english: The trouble is, the budget is insufficient.

examples.japanese: 努力はしたが、いかんせん、結果が出なかった。
examples.english: I made an effort, but regrettably, no result came.

pattern: Noun / V plain + にもほどがある
meaning_en: there's a limit to / (X) goes too far
meaning_detailed: 〜にもほどがある expresses criticism that X goes beyond acceptable bounds — 'there's a limit to X (and you've exceeded it)'. Often used as a strong complaint or scolding. Can follow nouns or plain forms. Neutral / casual register with emotional emphasis.
formation: Noun + にもほどがある; plain-form clause + にもほどがある.
formation_notes: Strong complaint — X has gone too far.
Common in criticism: 冗談にもほどがある ('there's a limit to jokes'), 嘘にもほどがある.
Neutral / conversational register with emotional emphasis.
Often paired with 失礼, 迷惑, 無礼.

examples.japanese: 冗談にもほどがある。
examples.english: There's a limit to joking — this has gone too far.

examples.japanese: 失礼にもほどがある。
examples.english: That's rude beyond limits.

examples.japanese: 遅刻にもほどがある。
examples.english: Being late has its limits — this is too much.

pattern: V ば-form / conditional + それまでだ
meaning_en: if X, that's the end / that's all there is to it
meaning_detailed: 〜ばそれまでだ expresses resignation or finality — 'if X happens, that's the end of everything'. Often used in warnings or hypothetical closings: 'if the battery dies, that's it'. Formal / neutral register with a resigned tone.
formation: V ば-form + それまでだ; also 〜たらそれまでだ.
formation_notes: Finality / resignation — 'if X, nothing more can be done'.
Common in warnings: 失敗すればそれまでだ.
Slightly formal / dramatic register.
The phrase それまでだ literally means 'until there' = 'that's the end'.

examples.japanese: 失敗すればそれまでだ。
examples.english: If you fail, that's the end of it.

examples.japanese: バッテリーが切れれば、それまでだ。
examples.english: If the battery dies, that's it.

examples.japanese: 諦めればそれまでだ。
examples.english: If you give up, that's the end.

pattern: i-adj 〜くもなんともない / na-adj + でもなんでもない
meaning_en: not even slightly / not at all
meaning_detailed: 〜くもなんともない / 〜でもなんでもない is an emphatic negation — 'not even slightly X' / 'not at all X'. Used to strongly deny that something has a quality. Conversational register, often in dismissive or surprised contexts.
formation: i-adj stem + くもなんともない; na-adj / noun + でもなんでもない.
formation_notes: Strong emphatic denial — 'not at all X, not even a bit'.
Common for dismissing claims: 寒くもなんともない ('not cold at all'), 大したことでもなんでもない ('nothing important at all').
Conversational register with emphasis.
Related to 〜なんて and 〜なんか as dismissive forms.

examples.japanese: 寒くもなんともない。
examples.english: I'm not cold at all.

examples.japanese: 大したことでもなんでもない。
examples.english: It's nothing important at all.

examples.japanese: 面白くもなんともなかった。
examples.english: It wasn't interesting at all.

pattern: Noun + に定評がある
meaning_en: is well-known for / has a solid reputation for
meaning_detailed: 〜に定評がある expresses that something or someone has an established, well-recognized reputation in a specific area — 'is known for X'. Common in reviews, introductions, and descriptions. 定評 (ていひょう) literally means 'established reputation'. Formal register.
formation: Noun + に定評がある.
formation_notes: Established reputation — for quality, skill, or trustworthiness.
Common in reviews and promotional descriptions.
Formal / neutral register.
The noun 定評 itself is a common word.

examples.japanese: この店は味に定評がある。
examples.english: This restaurant has a solid reputation for flavor.

examples.japanese: 彼女は演技に定評がある女優だ。
examples.english: She's an actress known for her acting skill.

examples.japanese: この会社は品質に定評がある。
examples.english: This company has a reputation for quality.

pattern: Noun / V dict-form + に足りない
meaning_en: not worth / insufficient for / unworthy of
meaning_detailed: 〜に足りない is a negation of 〜に足る (worth), expressing that something is 'not worth X' or 'unworthy of X'. More dismissive variant of 〜に足る. Common with nouns of evaluation: 信頼に足りない ('not worthy of trust'), 恐れるに足りない ('not worth fearing'). Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun / V dict-form + に足りない.
formation_notes: Negation of 〜に足る — 'not worth X'.
Common fixed phrases: 信頼に足りない, 恐れるに足りない, 取るに足りない.
Formal / literary register.
取るに足りない ('not worth mentioning') is a common fixed phrase.

examples.japanese: それは取るに足りないことだ。
examples.english: That's not worth mentioning.

examples.japanese: 彼は恐れるに足りない相手だ。
examples.english: He's not an opponent worth fearing.

examples.japanese: その情報は信頼するに足りない。
examples.english: That information is not worthy of trust.

pattern: Noun + を前にして
meaning_en: in front of / facing / on the verge of
meaning_detailed: 〜を前にして expresses that the main-clause action is done 'facing' or 'in front of' X — whether literally (in the presence of someone) or figuratively (facing an approaching event). Formal / literary register, common in dramatic narratives and formal descriptions.
formation: Noun + を前にして; attributive: 〜を前にした + noun.
formation_notes: Physical or temporal 'in front of / facing'.
Common with upcoming events: 試験を前にして ('with the exam approaching').
Also in front of audience: 観客を前にして ('before the audience').
Formal register.

examples.japanese: 試験を前にして、緊張している。
examples.english: Facing the exam, I'm nervous.

examples.japanese: 大勢の観客を前にして、彼は堂々と話した。
examples.english: Before a large audience, he spoke with confidence.

examples.japanese: 卒業を前にして、感慨深い。
examples.english: With graduation approaching, I'm deeply moved.

pattern: V た + り + もしない
meaning_en: doesn't even do / not even X
meaning_detailed: 〜たりもしない is an emphatic negation combining the listing form 〜たり with the negative — 'not even doing X' / 'doesn't even X'. Used to dismiss an action as not happening at all. Emphatic / casual register, often in complaints about someone's behavior.
formation: V た + り + もしない.
formation_notes: Emphatic negation — 'not even doing X'.
Often in complaints: 謝ったりもしない ('doesn't even apologize').
Conversational / emphatic register.
Builds on 〜たり (listing) with emphatic も and negation.

examples.japanese: 彼は謝ったりもしない。
examples.english: He doesn't even apologize.

examples.japanese: 連絡したりもしないで、勝手に来た。
examples.english: Without even contacting, he came on his own.

examples.japanese: 考えたりもしなかった。
examples.english: I didn't even think about it.

pattern: Noun + にありがち
meaning_en: common to / typical of (often with negative nuance)
meaning_detailed: 〜にありがち expresses that something is 'common / frequent / typical of' X — often with a slightly negative or critical nuance. Common in characterizations: 若者にありがち ('typical of young people'), 初心者にありがち ('common among beginners'). Conversational / neutral register.
formation: Noun + にありがち(な + noun / だ).
formation_notes: Typical occurrence — often with mild criticism or observation.
Common with groups and categories: 若者にありがち, 初心者にありがち.
Functions as a na-adjective: ありがちな話, ありがちだ.
Neutral / observational register.

examples.japanese: それは若者にありがちな失敗だ。
examples.english: That's a mistake typical of young people.

examples.japanese: 初心者にありがちな間違いを犯した。
examples.english: I made a mistake common among beginners.

examples.japanese: 現代人にありがちな悩みだ。
examples.english: It's a worry common to modern people.

pattern: Noun + そのもの
meaning_en: (X) itself / the very essence of
meaning_detailed: 〜そのもの expresses 'X itself' — emphasizing that something IS the essence or pure form of the preceding noun, not merely having X as a quality. Common in emphatic descriptions: 健康そのもの ('the picture of health / health itself'), 親切そのもの ('kindness itself'). Formal / neutral register.
formation: Noun + そのもの.
formation_notes: Emphatic identification — 'X itself / the very essence of X'.
Common with abstract nouns: 健康, 幸せ, 愛, 美しさ, 親切.
Formal / neutral register — emphatic but widely used.
Similar to 〜そのもの is the stronger 〜そのもの of

examples.japanese: 彼は親切そのものだ。
examples.english: He is kindness itself.

examples.japanese: 彼女は健康そのものだ。
examples.english: She is the picture of health.

examples.japanese: その景色は美しさそのものだった。
examples.english: That scenery was beauty itself.

pattern: V ます-stem / noun + かたがた
meaning_en: combining / at the same time / also as
meaning_detailed: 〜かたがた expresses that an action serves two purposes at once — 'combining X with Y' / 'taking the opportunity to do both'. Similar in function to 〜がてら but more formal. Often used in polite correspondence about visits or gifts: お礼かたがた ('serving also as thanks'). Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + かたがた; V ます-stem + かたがた.
formation_notes: Formal / polite 'serving two purposes at once'.
More formal than 〜がてら (N1 also).
Common in polite visits: お礼かたがた, ご挨拶かたがた.
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: お礼かたがた、ご挨拶に伺います。
examples.english: I will visit you for a greeting, which also serves as thanks.

examples.japanese: ご報告かたがた、お手紙を差し上げます。
examples.english: I'm sending a letter that also serves as a report.

examples.japanese: 散歩かたがた、郵便局へ行った。
examples.english: I went to the post office, which was also a walk.

pattern: Verb volitional + んとする
meaning_en: about to / trying to / on the verge of (literary)
meaning_detailed: 〜んとする is a literary / archaic construction meaning 'about to X' or 'trying to X'. Uses the classical volitional form with する. Very formal / literary, rarely in casual speech. Common fixed forms: 言わんとする ('trying to say'), 飛び去らんとする ('on the verge of flying away').
formation: V volitional (classical む/ん) + とする. For godan: 行かんとする. For ichidan: 食べんとする (or 食べようとする in modern form).
formation_notes: Literary / classical — modern equivalent is 〜ようとする.
Common fixed forms: 言わんとする, 消えんとする, 死なんとする.
Rare in casual speech — typical of literature and formal writing.
Attributive 〜んとする + noun is common: 言わんとするところ ('what one is trying to say').

examples.japanese: 彼の言わんとするところを理解した。
examples.english: I understood what he was trying to say.

examples.japanese: 太陽が沈まんとしている。
examples.english: The sun is about to set.

examples.japanese: 新たな時代が始まらんとしている。
examples.english: A new era is about to begin.

pattern: Plain form + に相違ない
meaning_en: no doubt / certainly / I'm sure
meaning_detailed: 〜に相違ない is a formal / literary equivalent of 〜に違いない ('must be'), expressing strong conviction — 'there is no doubt that X'. The noun 相違 literally means 'difference / discrepancy'. Very formal, common in written reports and formal analyses.
formation: V plain / i-adj + に相違ない; na-adj / noun + に相違ない.
formation_notes: Formal / literary equivalent of 〜に違いない.
Very common in formal writing: news, reports, essays.
Rare in casual speech — the modern equivalent is 〜に違いない.
Attached directly to nouns and na-adj without な/だ.

examples.japanese: 彼が犯人に相違ない。
examples.english: There's no doubt he's the culprit.

examples.japanese: この話は事実に相違ない。
examples.english: This story is certainly true.

examples.japanese: 成功するに相違ないと信じている。
examples.english: I believe he'll certainly succeed.

pattern: Noun + (が)ゆえの / ゆえの + noun
meaning_en: stemming from / due to (attributive)
meaning_detailed: 〜ゆえの is the attributive form of the literary ゆえに (because), used to modify a noun — 'a noun that stems from X'. Typically describes problems, consequences, or qualities that arise from a specific cause. Formal / literary register. The が is optional.
formation: Noun + (が)ゆえの + noun.
formation_notes: Attributive form of ゆえに — modifies a noun.
Common with problems or consequences: 貧しさゆえの苦労, 無知ゆえの失敗.
Literary / formal register.
The が is optional and adds emphasis.

examples.japanese: それは貧しさゆえの苦労だった。
examples.english: It was a hardship stemming from poverty.

examples.japanese: 若さゆえの失敗は、誰にでもある。
examples.english: Mistakes stemming from youth — everyone has them.

examples.japanese: 彼の成功は努力ゆえのものだ。
examples.english: His success is something stemming from effort.

pattern: Plain form / noun + とあっては
meaning_en: if it's (a case of) / given that it is
meaning_detailed: 〜とあっては expresses a conditional based on a specific circumstance — 'if it's the case that X, (then Y follows)'. Often used for commitments or required responses given a special situation. Close to 〜とあれば but with a slightly stronger case-making flavor. Formal / literary register.
formation: Noun + とあっては; plain-form clause + とあっては.
formation_notes: Conditional case-making — 'if it's a case of X, then Y'.
Close to 〜とあれば (covered at N2) — とあっては is slightly more insistent.
Main clause often expresses obligation or natural response.
Formal / literary register.

examples.japanese: 緊急事態とあっては、すぐ駆けつけなければならない。
examples.english: If it's an emergency, I have to rush over right away.

examples.japanese: 彼の頼みとあっては、断れない。
examples.english: If it's his request, I can't refuse.

examples.japanese: 子供のためとあっては、仕方がない。
examples.english: If it's for the children, it can't be helped.

pattern: Noun + に関わる
meaning_en: involves / affects / concerns (seriously)
meaning_detailed: 〜に関わる expresses that something seriously concerns or affects X — 'is a matter of X' / 'has bearing on X'. Often with consequential nouns: 命 ('life'), 名誉 ('honor'), 将来 ('future'), 信用 ('credit'). Implies weight or importance. Formal register.
formation: Noun + に関わる; attributive 〜に関わる + noun.
formation_notes: Weighty involvement — 'a matter of life / honor / future'.
Common nouns: 命, 名誉, 信用, 将来, 評判.
Formal register.
Distinct from 〜に関する (regarding, neutral) — 関わる implies serious impact.

examples.japanese: これは命に関わる問題だ。
examples.english: This is a matter of life and death.

examples.japanese: 会社の信用に関わるから、慎重に対応すべきだ。
examples.english: It concerns the company's credibility, so we should handle it carefully.

examples.japanese: 名誉に関わる事件が起こった。
examples.english: An incident affecting honor has occurred.

pattern: Plain form + ものを
meaning_en: if only / you should have (regret)
meaning_detailed: 〜ものを expresses the speaker's regret or frustration that someone didn't do what would have been sensible — 'if only you had X' / 'you should have X, but didn't'. Literary register, often with an emotional / scolding nuance. Common paired with 〜たら / 〜ば.
formation: V plain / i-adj + ものを; na-adj + な + ものを; noun + の + ものを.
formation_notes: Regret / frustration over someone's inaction or wrong action.
Often paired with conditional: 言ってくれたらよかったものを ('if only you had told me').
Literary / emotional register.
Distinct from 〜もの (reason) and 〜ものなら (if it were possible).

examples.japanese: 早く言ってくれればよかったものを。
examples.english: If only you had told me sooner.

examples.japanese: 素直に謝ればいいものを、彼は頑なだ。
examples.english: He should just apologize, but he's stubborn.

examples.japanese: 少し休めばよかったものを、無理してしまった。
examples.english: I should have rested a bit, but I pushed myself.

pattern: Noun / plain form + とみなす
meaning_en: regard as / consider as / deem to be
meaning_detailed: 〜とみなす expresses a formal judgment or designation — 'regard as X' / 'deem to be X'. Common in legal, business, and policy contexts for making categorical decisions. The verb 見なす literally means 'to regard / consider'. Formal / official register.
formation: Noun / plain form + とみなす (polite: とみなします); passive: とみなされる.
formation_notes: Formal / official designation — 'for the purposes of X, consider Y to be Z'.
Common in legal and policy: 〜とみなされる ('is deemed to be').
Formal / official register.
Related to 〜とみる (see as) but more categorical / final.

examples.japanese: 無断欠席は退学とみなされる。
examples.english: Unexcused absence will be regarded as withdrawal.

examples.japanese: 返事がない場合は、同意とみなします。
examples.english: If there's no reply, we will consider it as consent.

examples.japanese: 彼を犯人とみなすのは早すぎる。
examples.english: It's too early to regard him as the culprit.

pattern: V plain / noun + ではまいか / ではあるまいか
meaning_en: I wonder if / isn't it possibly
